payload.title: Total RNA Extraction from Tissue (TRIzol)
payload.description: A standard TRIzol-based total RNA extraction protocol suitable for most tissue types. Yields high-quality RNA for downstream RT-qPCR, RNA-seq, and microarray analysis.
payload.guidelines: Work in an RNase-free environment. Use filter tips throughout.
payload.before_start: Pre-chill centrifuge to 4°C. Prepare 75% ethanol with DEPC-treated water.
payload.warning: TRIzol contains phenol and guanidine isothiocyanate — use in a fume hood.

steps.step: Homogenise 50–100 mg tissue in 1 mL TRIzol Reagent using a tissue homogeniser. Pipette up and down until fully lysed.
Incubate homogenate at room temperature for 5 minutes to permit complete dissociation of nucleoprotein complexes.
Add 200 µL chloroform per 1 mL TRIzol. Cap securely and shake vigorously for 15 seconds. Incubate 2–3 minutes at RT.
Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C. The mixture separates into a lower red phenol-chloroform phase, an interphase, and a colourless upper aqueous phase containing RNA.
Transfer the aqueous phase (~500 µL) to a fresh RNase-free tube. Avoid disturbing the interphase.
Add 500 µL isopropanol. Mix by inversion and incubate 10 minutes at RT. Centrifuge 12,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C. RNA pellet should be visible.
Discard supernatant. Wash pellet with 1 mL 75% ethanol. Vortex briefly, centrifuge 7,500 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C. Repeat wash once.
Air-dry pellet for 5–10 minutes (do not over-dry). Resuspend in 30–50 µL DEPC-treated water. Incubate 55°C for 10 minutes to dissolve. Quantify by NanoDrop; expect A260/A280 ≥ 1.8.
